skill.name: json-to-llm-context
skill.description: Convert JSON or PostgreSQL jsonb payloads into compact, readable context for LLM prompts. The skill uses a local Python script to summarize entities, arrays, status fields, and nested details without dumping raw paths by default.
skill.summary: Convert noisy JSON payloads into concise, readable LLM context.
skill.author: vc999999999

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk because markdown backticks, ordinary descriptions, and heuristic keywords were misclassified as shell execution, weak crypto, sensitive key handling, reconnaissance, and obfuscation. Review found a local Python JSON summarizer with no network calls, no environment access, no subprocess execution, and no prompt injection text. The remaining risk is expected local script execution with user-directed file read and optional file write.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Local File Read and Write
low_findings.description: The Python script reads a user-specified input path and can write to a user-specified output path. This is expected for a local JSON conversion tool, but users should only run it on trusted paths and review output targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write calls are direct and clear. Risk is low because paths are supplied by the user and no exfiltration, privilege escalation, or hidden behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections in SKILL.md and references/rules.md are markdown inline code, code fences, and documentation examples. No Ruby backtick execution or shell execution API was found in the Python script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text or fenced examples. A targeted search found no subprocess, shell, system, popen, eval, or exec usage in the executable script.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to descriptions and headings, not cryptographic code. No md5, sha1, hashing routine, encryption function, or password handling was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines do not contain cryptographic operations. The script imports argparse, json, re, sys, Counter, dataclass, Path, and typing only.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Key File Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The sensitive certificate or key detections point to entity classification helper sets, not file names, certificates, private keys, or secret material. No evidence of credential collection or secret parsing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code only checks whether JSON objects look like users or orders based on field names. The flagged lines do not read certificates, keys, or secret files.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and Obfuscation Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are ordinary documentation and output examples. The obfuscation heuristic points to the script header, but the file is readable Python source with normal type hints and no encoded payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal skill instructions and readable Python. No host reconnaissance commands, encoded blobs, dynamic loaders, or prompt injection phrases were found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Reads and Writes User-Specified Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The script accepts input and output paths from command-line arguments. This is not malicious, but it should be disclosed because it can read local JSON files and overwrite an output file selected by the user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit in the script and required for the tool. It is listed as a disclosure pattern, not as evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Large JSON payloads waste tokens and hide the facts a model needs. This skill turns structured JSON or jsonb exports into concise, readable summaries for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Reads JSON from a file path or standard input.
Summarizes objects into Summary, Details, and Collections sections.
Condenses arrays with counts, status distributions, and sample records.
Preserves selected keys or dotted paths when requested.
Adds optional source path markers for traceability.
Supports flat and sectioned output styles.
limitations: It only handles valid JSON or jsonb-style JSON text.
It does not process PDFs, DOCX files, images, or prose documents.
It may omit low-signal empty values unless strict or preserve options are used.
Very large or deeply nested payloads may need tighter depth and sample limits.

use_cases.title: Summarize API Responses
use_cases.description: Convert a large service response into compact context before asking an LLM to debug behavior or explain state.

use_cases.title: Prepare jsonb Exports
use_cases.description: Turn database jsonb payloads into readable summaries for analysis, support review, or product investigation.

use_cases.title: Reduce Prompt Tokens
use_cases.description: Compress nested records while keeping identifiers, statuses, relationships, and important fields visible.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a JSON File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a local JSON file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use json-to-llm-context on this JSON file and return the readable summary.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Shorter Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this JSON into compact LLM context. Use fewer samples and shorter strings if the first output is too long.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve Important Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this payload, but preserve status, profile.email, identifiers, and order fields even when they look sparse.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Summary Lines
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for audits or debugging.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate sectioned context with path markers and expanded details so each summary line can be traced to the source JSON.

output_examples.input: A user profile payload with account status, contact details, roles, and timestamps.
output_examples.output: The result names the user, lists active status and verified contact information, and summarizes role counts in a collections section.

output_examples.input: An orders payload with several records, payment states, totals, and item counts.
output_examples.output: The result reports total orders, status distribution, and a few representative order examples without repeating every field.

output_examples.input: A nested configuration object with metadata, feature flags, and empty defaults.
output_examples.output: The result keeps meaningful settings, removes empty noise, and can preserve selected defaults when strict mode is requested.

best_practices: Use strict mode when missing false or empty values could change the meaning.
Preserve business-critical keys such as status, identifiers, emails, and relationship fields.
Lower sample, depth, and string limits before sending very large payloads to an LLM.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for non-JSON documents or OCR tasks.
Do not assume the summary preserves every source field unless strict and preserve options are used.
Do not run it on sensitive local files unless the output destination and sharing path are approved.

faq.question: Does this skill send JSON to a remote service?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found. The included script processes data locally.

faq.question: Can it read from standard input?
faq.answer: Yes. The script reads from standard input when no input file path is provided.

faq.question: Can it write the summary to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. It can write to a user-specified output path, or print to standard output.

faq.question: Will it preserve every JSON field?
faq.answer: No. It removes low-signal empty values by default, unless strict or preserve options are used.

faq.question: What formats are supported?
faq.answer: It supports valid JSON and PostgreSQL jsonb-style JSON text after parsing.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
